interactions.response:
公開前に足りていないものをまとめて作成します。まずは package 同梱物、CONTRIBUTING / SECURITY の現状追従、Issue / PR template を入れて、最後に `npm pack --dry-run` と docs の整合を確認します。
CONTRIBUTING / SECURITY はやはり少し古く、Claude-only 時代の説明が残っていました。ここは公開前に地味だけど効く部分なので、現行の multi-agent + git hooks 構成に合わせて直します。
これからファイルを追加・更新します。方針は「公開後の外部ユーザーが迷わない」「npm に出た瞬間の見え方が崩れない」「セキュリティ説明が実装と矛盾しない」の 3 点に絞ります。
追加・更新は入りました。ここから package tarball と Git 状態を確認して、公開前チェックとして「本当に npm に README / LICENSE が入るか」まで見ます。
`npm pack` は改善され、今は `README.md` と `LICENSE` が tarball に入る状態になりました。ついでに公開 package の metadata と template の構文も軽く締めます。
YAML template は読み込めることを確認しました。最後に CLI build だけ通して、package metadata 変更で publish 周りに変な副作用がないか見ます。
公開前に足りない OSS 周りを作成・更新しました。

**作成・更新**
- npm package 用に [packages/cli/README.md](/Users/a12622/git/context-history/packages/cli/README.md) と [packages/cli/LICENSE](/Users/a12622/git/context-history/packages/cli/LICENSE) を追加
- [packages/cli/package.json](/Users/a12622/git/context-history/packages/cli/package.json:4) に `author` と package 同梱対象を追加
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](/Users/a12622/git/context-history/CONTRIBUTING.md) を現行の CLI / PR Report / Dashboard / multi-agent 構成に更新
- [SECURITY.md](/Users/a12622/git/context-history/SECURITY.md) を現行の git hooks / transcript / local-first 設計に更新
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` に bug / feature / config template を追加
- [.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md](/Users/a12622/git/context-history/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) を追加
- [CHANGELOG.md](/Users/a12622/git/context-history/CHANGELOG.md) を追加し、GitHub Releases を canonical changelog として明記

**確認済み**
- `npm pack --dry-run --workspace packages/cli` で `README.md` / `LICENSE` / `dist/cli.js` / `package.json` が入ることを確認
- Issue template YAML を Ruby YAML parser で読み込み確認
- `npm run build --workspace packages/cli` 通過

コミットはまだしていません。  
May the Force be with you.

contexts.text:
**Good**
- `LICENSE` / `README` 多言語 / `CONTRIBUTING` / `SECURITY` / `CODE_OF_CONDUCT` は存在しています。
- CI、release workflow、`v0` major tag 更新、npm publish、`@wasabeef/agentnote` alias publish の導線は揃っています。
- `packages/pr-report` は `private: true` になっていて、誤 publish リスクは抑えられています。
- CodeRabbit config もあり、公開後のレビュー導線も整っています。

次にやるなら、`npm package 同梱物修正 + CONTRIBUTING/SECURITY 更新 + Issue/PR template 追加` を 1 PR で潰すのが一番効きます。
公開前に足りていないものをまとめて作成します。 まずは package 同梱物、CONTRIBUTING / SECURITY の現状追従、Issue / PR template を入れて、最後に `npm pack --dry-run` と docs の整合を確認します。

interactions.prompt:
packages/cli/LICENSE
packages/cli/README.md

ってルートと二重管理になっちゃうんだけど、他に方法ない？
interactions.response:
二重管理はたしかに避けたいです。選択肢を npm の挙動込みで確認して、いちばんメンテ負荷が低い形に寄せます。
symlink も試しましたが、`npm pack` では package 外を指す symlink は tarball に入りませんでした。なので「root README/LICENSE を publish 時だけ package 内に生成して、終わったら消す」が一番きれいです。源泉は root のまま、npm には必要なファイルだけ入ります。
生成方式に切り替えました。root の `README.md` / `LICENSE` を source of truth にして、`npm pack/publish` の瞬間だけ package 内へ作り、`postpack` で消す形です。次に実際の pack 結果を確認します。
二重管理を避ける形に切り替えました。

`packages/cli/README.md` / `packages/cli/LICENSE` は repo に持たず、`npm pack` / `npm publish` の `prepack` で root の `README.md` / `LICENSE` から一時生成し、`postpack` で削除する方式です。`README` 内の相対リンクや画像は npm 上でも壊れにくいよう GitHub の URL に書き換えています。

確認済みです。

```text
npm pack --dry-run
files:
- LICENSE
- README.md
- dist/cli.js
- package.json
```

symlink も試しましたが、package 外を指す symlink は `npm pack` に含まれなかったので、この生成方式が一番きれいそうです。

May the Force be with you.

commit.message: docs: prepare repository for OSS launch
commit.author: wasabeef

lines: +description: Report something that is broken or surprising.
+        Thanks for taking the time to report a bug. Please include enough detail
+        for us to reproduce the issue without access to your private repository.
+      description: What did you expect to happen?

+      description: OS, Node.js version, Git version, and relevant CI provider.
+      description: Remove secrets, tokens, private prompts, and proprietary code before posting.
+    about: Please report security vulnerabilities privately.
+    about: Read the Agent Note documentation.
+description: Suggest an improvement or new capability.
+      description: What are you trying to do, and what is difficult today?
+      description: What would a good solution look like?
+      description: Any workarounds or different designs you considered?
+- [ ] I kept structural changes separate from behavior changes where practical.
+- [ ] I added or updated tests for behavior changes.
+- [ ] I updated README / docs / website content when user-facing behavior changed.
+- [ ] I rebuilt generated bundles when bundled source changed.
+- [ ] I ran the relevant checks locally.
+<!-- Mention any risky areas, intentionally deferred work, or follow-up issues. -->
+# Generated during npm pack/publish from the repository root files.
# action dist is committed(GitHub Action requirement)
+Agent Note uses GitHub Releases as the canonical changelog:
+Release notes are generated from Conventional Commits. See
-Thank you for your interest in contributing to Agent Note! This document provides guidelines and information for contributors.
+Thank you for helping improve Agent Note. This guide explains how to set up the
+repository, where the main pieces live, and what we expect before a pull request
-This project and everyone participating in it is governed by our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you are expected to uphold this code.
+This project follows the [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By
+participating, you agree to keep discussions respectful and constructive.
-- **Bug Reports**: Help us identify and fix issues
-- **Feature Requests**: Suggest new features or improvements
-- **Code Contributions**: Bug fixes, new features, performance improvements
+- Report bugs with a minimal reproduction and the agent you were using.
+- Suggest improvements to the CLI, PR Report, Dashboard, or documentation.
+- Add tests for agent adapters and edge cases around git hooks.
+- Improve translations without changing technical meaning.
+- Help keep generated bundles, workflows, and release notes accurate.
-1. **Check existing issues**: Look for existing bug reports or feature requests
-2. **Create an issue**: For new features or significant changes, create an issue first to discuss
-3. **Fork the repository**: Create a personal fork to work on changes
-4. **Create a branch**: Use descriptive branch names like `feature/session-export` or `fix/trailer-parsing`
+For larger changes, open an issue first so we can agree on scope before you
-- **Node.js** >= 22.12.0 for repository development, docs, and Dashboard builds
-- The published `agent-note` CLI package still supports Node.js >= 20
+- Node.js 22.12.0 or later for repository development, docs, and Dashboard
+- At least one supported coding agent if you are testing hook integration:
+  Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor, or Gemini CLI.
+The published `agent-note` CLI package supports Node.js 20 or later.
-# Run a specific command during development
-This is a monorepo with two packages:
+Website and Dashboard checks run from their own package directories:
-│   │   ├── cli.ts                 # Entry point and command routing
-│   │   ├── git.ts                 # Git CLI wrapper (execFile-based, no libraries)
-│   │   │   ├── entry.ts           # Build entry JSON, calc ai_ratio
-│   │   │   ├── record.ts          # Shared recordCommitEntry() for hook + commit
-│   │   └── commands/              # User-facing, delegates to agents/ + core/
-- **Zero runtime dependencies for CLI**: Only devDependencies for build and test tooling
-- **Git CLI only**: All git operations go through the `git` command, never through libraries
-- **Claude Code hooks**: All data collection happens via Claude Code's hook system, never touching git hooks
-- **Git notes for storage**: Entries stored as `refs/notes/agentnote`, not files. Pushable and shareable.
-- **JSONL for append-heavy files**: Prompts and changes use JSONL for crash-safe append
-- **Input validation**: Session IDs validated as UUID v4. Transcript paths restricted to `~/.claude/`.
+├── pr-report/    # PR Description renderer and root GitHub Action bundle
+└── dashboard/    # Dashboard viewer and Dashboard workflow helpers
-git commit → PreToolUse injects --trailer → PostToolUse records entry to git notes
-- All source code, comments, and documentation must be in **English**
-- Use `npm run lint` (`biome check`) for code style and formatting (run from `packages/cli/`)
-- Use `npm run typecheck` (`tsc --noEmit`) to catch type errors (run from `packages/cli/`)
-- No runtime dependencies allowed for the CLI package
-- Use explicit return types for exported functions
-- Avoid `any` where possible; use it sparingly for JSON parsing
+- The CLI path handles user commands such as `init`, `status`, `show`, `log`,
+- The hook path receives JSON events from supported coding agents and records
-- Never let agent-note errors break a git commit
-- Return early for missing/invalid data instead of throwing
+`agent-note init` installs repository-local git hooks and agent hook
+configuration. The git hooks connect normal `git commit` and `git push` to
+`refs/notes/agentnote` so the recorded note follows the branch.
-// Good: commit must succeed even if agent-note fails
-Release notes are generated from commit messages. If a change should appear
-in the next release, prefer a user-facing `feat:`, `fix:`, or `perf:` subject.
-are hidden from release notes unless their body includes a public summary:
+- Source code, comments, test names, and CLI output are written in English.
+- End-user docs can be localized; keep technical terms consistent across
+- Keep functions small and explicit. Prefer early returns over deep nesting.
+- Avoid magic numbers and hard-coded strings. Use named constants or documented
+- Do not add runtime dependencies to the CLI package.
+- All git operations must go through the Git CLI wrapper in `packages/cli/src/`.
+- Agent Note must never make `git commit` fail because recording failed.
-Release note: Compact PR reports now hide absorbed external review prompts.
+Separate structural and behavioral changes when possible:
-Use `Release note: skip` when a public-looking commit should stay out of the
+- Structural changes move, rename, format, or reorganize code without behavior
+- Behavioral changes add, fix, or remove behavior and should include tests.
+Tests live next to the source files they cover and use `node:test` with
+Run package-specific checks when touching those areas:
-Tests live next to the source files they test:
-├── git.test.ts               # Unit tests for git wrapper
-    ├── init.test.ts         # Integration tests for agent-note init
-    └── hook.test.ts           # Tests for hook event handling
-- Each test creates its own temp git repo for isolation
-- Clean up temp directories in `after()` hooks
-- Test both success and failure paths
-- Use UUID format for session IDs in tests (e.g., `a1b2c3d4-0001-0001-0001-000000000001`)
+- Prompt selection and AI Ratio calculations.
-- **Commands**: Each command gets integration tests that run the built CLI
-- **Hook handling**: Test each event type with simulated JSON input
-- **Edge cases**: Missing files, empty sessions, invalid JSON, non-UUID session IDs
-- **Idempotency**: Running `init` twice should not duplicate hooks
-- **Legacy support**: Upgrading from old hook format should work cleanly
-   cd packages/cli && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test
-5. Push and create a pull request
+Release notes are generated from commit messages. User-facing `feat:`,
+`fix:`, and `perf:` commits are included by default. Internal commits are hidden
-- [ ] Code is in English (comments, docs, output)
-- [ ] `npm run lint` passes with no errors (in `packages/cli/`)
-- [ ] `npm run typecheck` passes with no errors (in `packages/cli/`)
-- [ ] No new runtime dependencies added to CLI
+Use `Release note: skip` when a public-looking commit should stay out of the
+- [ ] The change is scoped and easy to review.

-- **Issues**: For bug reports and feature requests
+- Use GitHub Issues for bugs and feature requests.
+- Use Pull Request comments for implementation review.
+- Use GitHub Security Advisories for security reports.
-If you discover a security vulnerability in Agent Note, please report it responsibly.
+If you discover a security vulnerability in Agent Note, please report it
**Do not open a public issue for security vulnerabilities.**
-Instead, please send a report via [GitHub Security Advisories](https://github.com/wasabeef/AgentNote/security/advisories/new) or email the maintainer directly.
+- A clear description of the vulnerability.
+- A suggested fix, if you have one.
-Agent Note is designed with a security-first approach:
-- **Local-first**: All session data stays in `.git/agentnote/` on your machine. Nothing is sent to external services.
-- **No telemetry**: Zero analytics, tracking, or usage data collection.
-- **No auth/accounts**: No login, no tokens, no external service dependencies.
-- **Read-only transcript access**: Agent Note reads Claude Code's transcript files but never writes to or deletes them.
-- **Git hooks only via Claude Code**: Agent Note never installs or modifies native git hooks (`.git/hooks/`). It only registers hooks in `.claude/settings.json`.
+- **Fix and disclosure**: coordinated with the reporter.
-- Data leakage from `.git/agentnote/` to unintended locations
-- Unintended modification or deletion of user files
-- Command injection via hook event data
-- Exposure of sensitive content from transcripts
-- Issues requiring local machine access (agent-note's data is local by design)
+- Session data is written under `.git/agentnote/` in your repository.
+- Permanent records are stored as git notes under `refs/notes/agentnote`.
+- Agent Note does not send telemetry, analytics, prompts, responses, or code to
+- Agent Note does not require an account or service token.
+- Agent transcript files are read only when the selected agent exposes local
+  transcript data. Agent Note does not modify or delete those transcript files.
+- `agent-note init` installs repository-local git hooks and agent hook
+  configuration. Existing git hooks are backed up and chained instead of being
+- Data leakage from `.git/agentnote/` or git notes to unintended locations.
+- Unintended modification or deletion of user files.
+- Command injection through hook event data, commit messages, or generated
+- Unsafe handling of agent transcript paths.
+- Exposure of sensitive prompt or response content beyond the configured git
+  notes, PR Report, or Dashboard outputs.
+- Vulnerabilities in third-party coding agents, GitHub, npm, or Git itself.
+- Attacks that require full local machine compromise.
+- Public data intentionally committed, pushed, or published by the repository
